content_body:
You know summertime is here when you can get fresh-grown produce, homemade jams and jellies, honey, farm-fresh eggs, and so much more at the North Georgia Farmers Market!

Located in the parking lot of Cohutta Country Store on Hwy 515 right outside of Blue Ridge. 

An array of goat cheese, farm-fresh cheeses, locally grown mushrooms, heirloom seed starts, locally grown fresh vegetables, grass-fed beef, and pork, as well as local artisan products, are all neatly displayed at the outdoor farmers market.

Above all, the best part about the North Georgia Farmers Market is what makes Blue Ridge unique from all the rest: the people. 

The farmers market is the perfect representation of everything that is Blue Ridge.

It is quaint, friendly, rustic, beautiful, and everything local.

The farmers market, as is many of the events in Blue Ridge, is year-round every Saturday from 9-12.
title: North Georgia Farmers Market
